name: Roger Wolfe Kahn
abstract: Roger Wolfe Kahn (October 19, 1907 – July 12, 1962) was an American jazz and popular musician, composer, bandleader (Roger Wolfe Kahn and His Orchestra) and an aviator.

concerts.label: Artists Series of The Association of Music School Settlements

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I think the first one was with Roger Wolfe Kahn... I was just a jazz soloist in the band, and Roger was a marvelous guy to work for.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Waters
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Roger Wolfe Kahn? ... [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, I liked, at that time I enjoyed the Elk [?], very much those bands for their musical ability.

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: Another guy was Roger Kahn, he wound up over here at Grumman's. ... Roger Wolfe, he was a test pilot there.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: Hannah Williams married Roger Wolfe Kahn the international banker's son.

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: we used to entertain Roger Wolfe Kahn, Paul Whiteman, Henry Bussv... It was a small nightclub called The Nest... We played the Nest
